name: Grandmother's Fruitcake
recipeInstructions:
1. MAKE a liner for a 10-inch tube pan by drawing an 18-inch circle on brown paper (not recycled). Cut out the circle and set the pan in the center, drawing around the base of the pan and inside the tube. Remove the pan and fold the circle into eighths with the lines on the outside.
2. CUT off the pointed tip of the triangle along the line. Unfold the paper and cut along the folds to the outside line. Place the liner in the pan and grease it, then set aside. Repeat this procedure for the second pan.
3. COMBINE 1/2 cup flour, dates, and the next five ingredients (ground nutmeg, ground cinnamon, ground allspice, baking powder) in a large bowl, tossing gently to coat.
4. BEAT butter at medium speed with an electric mixer until creamy. Gradually add sugar, beating well. Add eggs, beating until blended after each addition. Add milk and corn syrup, mixing well.
5. COMBINE the remaining 3 1/2 cups flour, baking soda, and the next four ingredients (brandy, ground nutmeg, ground cinnamon, ground allspice) in a separate bowl. Add to the butter mixture alternately with 1 cup brandy, beginning and ending with the flour mixture. Mix at low speed after each addition until blended.
6. POUR the batter into the prepared pans. Place pecan halves in flower designs on top of the batter if desired.
7. BAKE at 350°F for 1 hour or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean. Remove from oven and cool completely in pans on wire racks.
8. REMOVE cakes from pans, peeling away the paper. Wrap in brandy-soaked cheesecloth and store in airtight containers in a cool place. Pour a little brandy over the cakes each week for at least one month before serving. Place cherry halves in the center of pecan flowers if desired.
